title: Misopates calycinum
taxonomy: Misopates calycinum is a species of plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and the class Magnoliopsida. The order it falls under is Lamiales. Misopates calycinum is further classified as a member of the genus Misopates. Within the classification, it is considered a type of flowering plant.

intro: Misopates calycinum, also known as Lophosia calycinum, is a flowering plant species in the family Plantaginaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Misopates calycinum include iridoid glycosides, such as ajugoside and leucoside, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Misopates calycinum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat colds, sore throats, and other respiratory ailments.
